sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः । एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
hindiTranslation: इच्छा, द्वेष, सुख, दुःख, संघात, चेतना प्राणशक्ति और धृति  इन विकारोंसहित यह क्षेत्र संक्षेपसे कहा गया है ॥
englishTranslation: Desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the body, consciousness, and steadiness: this field, together with its changes, is briefly described.
essence: Your reactions belong to the field, not to the knower.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The field includes the body, feelings, impulses, and the mind's steadiness. All of these are part of what changes, not the knower behind them.
simpleInsight: You can stop confusing your moods and reactions with who you are.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna begins separating what changes from what truly knows. After naming the field's basic parts, Krishna now includes desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, and steadiness as part of the same changing nature.
modernRelevance: You feel a surge of attraction, then irritation, then numbness, and call it your identity. This verse asks you to see those shifts as passing states, not your core.
translationLiteral: इच्छा (desire) / द्वेषः (aversion) / सुखं (pleasure) / दुःखं (pain) / सङ्घातः (the body as a composite) / चेतना (consciousness) / धृतिः (steadiness) / एतत् (this) / क्षेत्रम् (field) / समासेन (briefly) / सविकारम् (with changes) / उदाहृतम् (is described)
